Not long enough Henry’s mother has a life sentence

Kiran Kaur removed the dagger used to stab defenceless student Mr Nowak to death from the crime scene after her son, Vickrum Digwa, launched an unprovoked attack. Digwa, 23, was jailed for life last month for murdering Mr Nowak, 18, who died in police handcuffs on a night out in December last year after a chance encounter with his assailant. She was handed a three-year jail term as she appeared at Southampton Crown Court for her sentencing hearing today.

Oh, I'm old enough to remember Labour in the 70s. We are all going to be doomed if Burnham is taking us back there. Doomed, I say........

Andy Burnham has been unveiled as the winner of the bizarre one-candidate contest at an event at the TUC headquarters in London , watched by fawning would-be Cabinet ministers. In his speech, Mr Burnham - who ditched his trademark black t-shirt for a suit and tie - harked back to a time before the 'wrong turns' of the 1980s as his 'coronation' at the top of the party concludes. He hinted at nationalisations and more public control, telling activists he would 'return to a Labour they once knew' and 'eradicate' Reform. He said the movement was 'forged in the steelworks' and dockyards, committing to end 'Neoliberalism' - a vague term often used on the Left to denounce capitalism.
  • 16k comments
  • 4 videos

To find out what indeed had happened to make Elaine McGowan the target of a Caribbean hit job, the Daily Mail visited the quaint village of Saltwood on the south coast of England - where until last summer she was the landlady of the local pub. The broad daylight murder of July 5 is, unsurprisingly, the talk of the town, with various rumours and theories shared through hushed tones on street corners. According to her sister Joanne Butcher, Mrs McGowan - the daughter of Caribbean immigrants who had settled in South London in the 1960s - was outside her godfather's house when the incident happened. I can guarantee this is pure fiction!

'For goodness' sake, pull it up over your chin,' I bark for the third or fourth time since leaving Kyiv. The subject complies, good-naturedly. An olive-green Ukrainian military balaclava I'd bought for the purpose of disguising former British prime minister Boris Johnson is adjusted to my satisfaction. His Mr Punch profile, I'd realised, is almost as distinctive as his famously out-of-control blond thatch. Recognition by strangers, however well-meaning, could put us all at even greater risk on this journey to Ukraine's eastern frontline. To my further, possibly woke, exasperation he now bellows - in those distinctive fruity tones - what has become his balaclava-donning mantra: 'Burka! Burka! Burka!'

I've been on mounjaro for sixteen months and used 10 different suppliers for cost reasons. I've saved a considerable sum as I stored quite a few pens before the Trump price rise in August 25 and made use of the golden dose for the last six pens. I have four months supply in my fridge now as I have found further competitive prices. I expect to be finished after my current stock and will switch to lower dose Mounjaro pills for maintenance. I've lost 8 stone so far and will fight to protect my weight loss. I qualified for an NHS prescription but the waiting list was likely to be a year or more and my health could not wait. It has still been an expensive process but worth it. No regrets. I hope those patients that desperately need help who cannot afford to pay get what is needed. It's a real game changer!

Two young farm workers have been hailed as heroes after they put their lives and machinery at risk to stop a wildfire spreading to a housing estate. Jay Loft, 22, and Cameron Bartram, 19, reacted quickly when a blaze spread over 750 yards across wheat fields in the Woodthorpe area of York on Sunday afternoon. The duo had been working at a nearby site when they were called about the fire - and with landowner Russell Wagstaff 45 minutes away, they sprang into action.

The typically stoic Anne, 75, couldn't conceal her amusement as she came face-to-face with a humanoid robot during an official three-day trip to South Korea. He carried out CPR (he used to be a medic in the army) but she was gone. We take comfort from the fact that it was a peaceful death although traumatic for us the family.

Nothing warms the heart like a happy beaver.

The fugitive rodent, named Steve McQueen for his escaping exploits, made multiple attempts to escape from his enclosure since arriving at Wallington in 2023. Rangers suspected that Steve had females on his mind, which caused him to make a cunning getaway on three separate occasions. Now he has left his life of crime behind after finding love at a new countryside home in Wales - where he has become a father for the first time.
  • 38 comments

well done hope you had a realy nice day out

Claire Howard, from Birmingham, flew to Beauvais, France, for a relaxing day of food, sightseeing and sunshine and was home before midnight. Explaining how she pulled it off, the 48-year-old revealed she spotted return Ryanair flights on Skyscanner for just £27 and booked them a month in advance. 'I've been to Paris a few times and didn't want to travel in from Beauvais as Paris is really pricey and busy,' Claire said.
